message.reasoning_content:
We need to classify the trajectory based on the evidence card. The trajectory is from LunarLander-v2. Features: episode length 71, terminated true, truncated false. initial_distance 1.412, final_distance 0.196, distance_improvement 1.216, final_speed 1.011, final_angle_abs 0.152, contact_ratio_last20 0.1 (low). reward components: approach_progress positive, velocity_control 0, angle_stability negative, landing_bonus 0, crash_penalty 0, survival_bonus. Rule label says partial_progress with detail approach_unstable.

Check success criteria: both legs must contact, lander near pad (x near 0). final_legs_contact_both feature not directly provided but contact_ratio_last20=0.1 suggests legs only contact 10% of last 20 steps, likely not both at termination. Also final_speed is high (1.011), final_angle is 0.152 (moderate but not huge). But high speed and low leg contact indicate not stable landing. Distance improvement shows progress. So partial_progress is appropriate, not success_like.

Thus agree with rule. Confidence high (0.85). Use for memory and TAGE pair, allowed role mid_reference.

Evidence used: distance_improvement, final_speed, contact_ratio, final_angle.

No conflicts.
